item.title: Descriptors & Decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DesôÙcripôÙtors are an amaôÙzing tool to haôÙve in our toolôÙboôÙx, as they coôÙme in handôÙy in many opporôÙtuôÙniôÙtieôÙs.

ProôÙbaôÙbly the best thing about desôÙcripôÙtorôÙs, is that they can imôÙproôÙve otheôÙr ôÙsoôÙluôÙtionôÙs. LeôÙt's see how we can wriôÙte beôÙtter deôÙcoôÙraôÙtorôÙs, by using desôÙcripôÙtorôÙs.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 5 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Types of Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
ReôÙsuôÙming from wheôÙre we leôÙft off, on the preôÙvious posôÙt, on whiôÙch we took A first look at desôÙcripôÙtors, it's tiôÙme to exôÙploôÙre their diôÙffeôÙrent tyôÙpes and how they woôÙrk inôÙterôÙnaôÙllôÙy.

In Python, almost everything is represented with a dictionary. Objects are dictionaries. Classes are objects, hence they also are contained into a dictionary. This is denoted by the __dict__ attribute that objects have.

There are two types of descriptors: data descriptors and non-data ones. If a descriptor implements both [1] __get__() and __set__(), it's called a data descriptor; otherwise is a non-data descriptor.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 7 minutos de lectura)

item.title: A first look at descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DesôÙcripôÙtors are one of the most poôÙwerôÙful feaôÙtuôÙres of PyôÙthoôÙn. The reaôÙson whôÙy ôÙtheôÙy'ôÙre so poôÙwerôÙful is beôÙcauôÙse they enaôÙble us to conôÙtrol the coôÙre opeôÙraôÙtionôÙs (ôÙgeôÙt, seôÙt, deôÙleôÙte) [1], of an attriôÙbuôÙte in a giôÙven obôÙjecôÙt, so that we can hook a parôÙtiôÙcuôÙlar coôÙde, conôÙtroôÙlled by us, in orôÙder to moôÙdiôÙfy, chanôÙge, or exôÙtend the oôÙriôÙgiôÙnal opeôÙraôÙtioôÙn.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 8 minutos de lectura)
